name: Endpoint Validity appraisal for Tad Safety Interactions Adverse Events bundle
summary: Accepted. The staged endpoints are valid for a safety-boundary baseline: label contraindications and adverse reactions, blood-pressure interaction outcomes, hypotension-related treatment-emergent adverse events, MACE, and serious adverse events. The claim does not convert surrogate hemodynamic endpoints, voluntary postmarketing signals, or no-detected-SAE results into proof of safety for off-label healthy users.

findings.claim_or_issue: Standing blood-pressure outliers and mean maximal standing systolic blood-pressure decreases are surrogate endpoints, but they are valid for nitrate and alpha-blocker interaction boundary claims.
findings.why_it_matters: These endpoints support caution and contraindication logic without requiring clinical event endpoints for the same narrow interaction question.
findings.recommended_action: Keep the endpoint language tied to hemodynamic interaction risk, not broad cardiovascular-outcome benefit or harm estimates.

findings.claim_or_issue: Postmarketing serious signals, MACE, and serious adverse events are not deeply characterized enough in this baseline to estimate rare-event incidence for healthy off-label users.
findings.why_it_matters: Low observed or unquantified event rates can be misread as absence of risk when denominators, event definitions, and follow-up are incomplete.
findings.recommended_action: Preserve the staged caveat that rare-event signals and absolute-risk estimates remain uncertain until deeper full-text extraction or pharmacovigilance review is added.
